title: Workbuddy Workflow (Batch 3 â€” issue #204)
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of .github/workbuddy/workflows/*.md. The state machine itself lives in a fenced ```yaml code block in the Markdown body and is parsed by the loader, not this schema (see $comment). As of batch 3, the runtime synthesizes the agent prompt's transition footer from each state's transitions map; agent prompt bodies no longer author label-flip instructions. Batch 4 adds rollout state options inside the fenced YAML block (`rollouts`, `join.strategy: rollouts`, `join.min_successes`). v0.6.0 also adds `mode: review|synthesize` on state entries in that same fenced YAML block. Those body fields are accepted by the strict workflow loader alongside this frontmatter schema. Workflow markdown OUTSIDE the fenced YAML block is plain documentation â€” Go template expressions like {{.Issue.Number}} are NOT evaluated and will trip linter code WB-L003. Keep this in sync with internal/config/types.go (WorkflowConfig).
$comment: JSON Schema validates workflow frontmatter, while the strict Go loader validates the fenced state-machine YAML (including rollout keys, mode=review|synthesize, and unknown-key rejection) because those fields live inside the Markdown body. Footer injection happens entirely at the prompt-rendering boundary in internal/runtime; workflows do not declare a custom footer template. The validate-docs / validate config path treats either frontmatter-schema or body-YAML violations as CI failures.

issue_label.description: Issue label that activates this workflow. Two workflows must not share the same trigger label.

max_retries.description: Upper bound on state revisits before the runtime records retry/failure intent.

max_review_cycles.description: Orchestrator-level cap on devâ†”review round-trips (developingâ†’reviewingâ†’developing). On cap-hit the Coordinator stops dispatching dev-agent and review-agent, posts a needs-human comment with a rejection-trail digest, and emits dev_review_cycle_cap_reached. 0 (or unset) inherits the default of 3.
